sourceMovie.title: Live and Let Die
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Live and Let Die works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Casino Royale
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Live and Let Die clicked for you, Casino Royale (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Live and Let Die clicked for you, Spectre (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Live and Let Die?
faq.answer: Try Casino Royale, Spectre, Dr. No—each shares DNA with Live and Let Die while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Live and Let Die?
faq.answer: Live and Let Die (1973) is best described as Adventure, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Live and Let Die part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "James Bond Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
